mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls reflect labor market expansion, increasing aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, which pushes S&P 500 prices higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS increases but SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of instances over a 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate by more than 25 basis points within one month signals an imminent easing cycle, lowering discount rates and boosting equity valuations, resulting in positive SPX forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines by more than 25bp in one month
failure_condition: If after such a drop, SPX 20-day returns are negative in over 60% of occurrences or rank IC is below 0.03 over any 2-year window, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in industrial production and retail sales indicate a synchronized demand‑supply expansion, reducing inventory risks and boosting corporate profits, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If when both series rise, SPX 60-day returns are negative in at least 50% of episodes over a 5‑year period, or the interaction term in a regression is insignificant (p>0.10), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is already below 4%, further declines signal an overheating labor market that compresses profit margins through wage inflation, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 4% and then declines further by at least 0.1pp in one month
failure_condition: If under this regime, SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of cases or the average return is positive, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts above 1,500 thousand units (annualized) within two months signals robust residential investment and consumer confidence, boosting economic growth expectations and driving S&P 500 prices higher over the following month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases by more than 100k units in two months
failure_condition: If after such a surge SPX 20-day returns are negative in over 50% of cases or the average excess return over risk‑free rate is negative, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales indicate strong consumer spending, which directly boosts corporate revenues and earnings expectations, pushing S&P 500 prices higher over a short one‑week horizon.
failure_condition: If RSAFS increase is accompanied by negative SPX 5-day returns in more than 60% of observations over any 2‑year rolling window, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent high real interest rates (FEDFUNDS high) and low unemployment (UNRATE low) create a restrictive monetary stance relative to the output gap, dampening investment and consumption, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If when both conditions hold simultaneously, SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 60% of episodes or the interaction coefficient in a regression has t‑statistic below 1.96, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in industrial production below the 100 index level signals a contraction in manufacturing activity, foreshadowing a broader economic slowdown that reduces corporate earnings and leads to negative S&P 500 returns within the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 100 and remains below for at least two months
failure_condition: If after such a decline SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances or the average return is above zero, the hypothesis is falsified.
